mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL remains positive on a monthly basis, the market infers prolonged policy accommodation and weak real-rate pressure, which supports a decline in the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 10-year yields rise in triggered months despite FEDFUNDS staying near the lower bound and CPIAUCSL remaining positive, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 7.5% signals sustained labor-market slack and weaker expected demand, which pushes investors toward duration and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are nonnegative in more than 60% of months when UNRATE is above 7.5%, or if the conditional average yield change is not negative after threshold crossings.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve over the prior month, investors infer firmer nominal spending and payroll demand, which lifts term premium expectations and raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not coincide with positive 5-day yield changes more often than not, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a sharp industrial slump, a falling INDPRO combined with collapsing HOUST signals a broad output gap and weaker inflation pressure, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its prior 3-month average and HOUST is declining
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in INDPRO and HOUST is followed by flat or rising 60-day yields in a majority of occurrences, or if the combined signal underperforms either single proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid downward move in CPIAUCSL reduces inflation compensation in Treasury pricing, so the 10-year yield falls over the next month when inflation is decelerating on a broad monthly basis.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation deceleration episodes are not followed by lower yields more than 55% of the time, or if the average 20-day yield change is not negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak while RSAFS does not recover, the market prices a durable demand shortfall and expects lower policy rates, which pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is negative or flat versus the prior quarter and RSAFS remains below its 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if weak-GDP episodes with nonrecovering retail sales do not precede negative 60-day yield changes, or if the signal loses significance after revision-adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: A continued rise in UNRATE alongside slowing PAYEMS marks worsening labor-market slack and weaker income growth, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth is negative over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous unemployment increases and payroll losses are followed by nonnegative 20-day yield changes in more than 40% of cases, or if the joint signal is weaker than single-variable baselines.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led contraction, falling HOUST with weakening INDPRO signals shrinking real activity and lower inflation pass-through, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent housing starts and industrial production weakness does not lead to negative 5-day yield moves more often than chance, or if the interaction effect disappears outside recessionary months.
